sessions.intent: Implement crypto module with pluggable provider architecture (Engine.Providers)

before.assumptions: Test stubs and plan already exist from test-designer. Identity module patterns are the reference for handler structure.
before.risk: Low — self-contained module with no dependencies on existing modules

sessions.plan: Implement ICryptoProvider interface, DefaultProvider (keccak256/sha256), HashedData type, hash handler, verify handler. Fill in test stubs. Skip bcrypt tests. Add Engine.Providers as generic pluggable implementation registry.

actions.context: Core crypto module: interface, default provider, types, hash and verify action handlers. Handlers are thin — resolve provider, serialize data, delegate to provider.

Engine.Providers — type-keyed ConcurrentDictionary<Type, object> for pluggable module implementations. Solves Foundation Checklist item #4 (pluggable action implementations). PLang developers override defaults by loading DLLs that implement the same provider interface.
Replaced 15 stub tests with real implementations. Added [Skip] to 4 bcrypt tests. All 26 active tests pass. Tests use Engine.Providers.Register<ICryptoProvider>() for mock injection.
Updated Foundation Checklist item #4 to DONE. Added todo for retiring Libraries as central action registry.

after.notes: Design evolved during session: plan called for DataSource provider resolution, but DataSource serializes to JSON. Discussed with Ingi → MemoryStack → then designed Engine.Providers as the proper generic solution for pluggable module implementations. Todo added for retiring Libraries.
